meta.title: BST Particle Family
meta.description: Every Standard Model particle described as what it IS on the substrate S^2 x S^1 with configuration space D_IV^5. Not what it does — what it IS.

the_one_cycle: Light is emitted -> touches the universe -> brings back information -> information is stored -> the substrate emits light -> the cycle continues.

particles.name: Photon
particles.substrate_description: Phase oscillation on S^1 with zero net winding number. The substrate communicating with itself. The verb of the universe.
particles.cycle_role: Messenger — carries phase information between committed contacts at speed c
particles.key_insight: Speed c is a tautology: one contact per causal step. Zero mass because zero topological cost.

particles.name: Electron
particles.mass_formula: m_e = boundary Bergman mode (below Wallach set, k=1)
particles.substrate_description: One complete winding around S^1. The simplest thing the substrate can do that persists. A boundary excitation on the Shilov boundary S^4 x S^1, NOT in the bulk.
particles.cycle_role: Simplest persistent circuit — the universe's first committed act beyond communication
particles.key_insight: Light because it lives on the boundary, not in the bulk. The note played on the edge of the drum.

particles.name: Positron
particles.substrate_description: Anti-winding on S^1 (n = +1). The circuit running backward. When electron meets positron: n=-1 + n=+1 -> n=0, two photons. The winding unwinds.
particles.cycle_role: Anti-circuit — annihilation partner
particles.key_insight: Annihilation is topological: opposite windings cancel to zero.

particles.name: Muon
particles.substrate_description: Electron circuit threaded through three complex dimensions (D_IV^3 embedding). Not a fat electron — a deeper threading. Mass ratio is the Bergman Jacobian of the embedding.
particles.cycle_role: Second-generation persistent circuit
particles.key_insight: Each generation threads through progressively more dimensions. More dimensions = more Bergman weight = heavier.

particles.name: Tau
particles.substrate_description: Electron circuit at full D_IV^5 embedding depth. The heaviest lepton because it threads all five complex dimensions.
particles.cycle_role: Third-generation persistent circuit
particles.key_insight: Mass extends muon formula by (g/N_c)^{10/3} = (7/3)^{10/3} correction.

particles.name: Electron neutrino
particles.substrate_description: The Z_3 Goldstone mode — the vacuum ground state in propagating form. Not a circuit, not a winding. The vacuum expressing its geometry. What a channel looks like in its ground state while maintaining topological connectivity.
particles.cycle_role: The vacuum checking on itself — substrate confirming its own coherence
particles.key_insight: Massless neutrino IS the vacuum. Dirac, not Majorana — no neutrinoless double-beta decay.

particles.name: Muon neutrino
particles.substrate_description: First vacuum excitation above the ground state. The genus g=7 enters via generation structure in the boundary seesaw.
particles.cycle_role: First excited vacuum propagation mode
particles.key_insight: Boundary seesaw: m_e (boundary) plays Dirac role, m_p (bulk) plays Majorana role, two factors of alpha from electroweak vertices.

particles.name: Tau neutrino
particles.substrate_description: Second vacuum excitation. dim_R/N_c = 10/3 where dim_R is the real dimension of CP^2.
particles.cycle_role: Second excited vacuum propagation mode
particles.key_insight: Mass ratio m_nu3/m_nu2 = 40/7 = 5.714, observed ~5.79 (1.4%).

particles.name: Up quark
particles.mass_formula: Light quark (constituent mass from chiral condensate)
particles.substrate_description: 2/3 of an S^1 winding + 1/3 of a Z_3 circuit on CP^2. A partial circuit that only makes sense when all three quarks complete the closure.
particles.cycle_role: Partial color circuit — component of baryons and mesons
particles.key_insight: Fractional charge 2/3 follows from Z_3 closure requirement: three quarks must sum to integer S^1 winding.

particles.name: Down quark
particles.mass_formula: Light quark (constituent mass from chiral condensate)
particles.substrate_description: -1/3 of an S^1 winding + 1/3 of a Z_3 circuit on CP^2. Partner to the up quark in the first generation.
particles.cycle_role: Partial color circuit — heavier first-gen partner
particles.key_insight: Down heavier than up -> neutron heavier than proton -> free neutron decays -> hydrogen universe.

particles.name: Charm quark
particles.mass_formula: Up quark at D_IV^3 embedding depth
particles.substrate_description: Up quark circuit threaded through D_IV^3 embedding. Second-generation color circuit.
particles.cycle_role: Second-generation partial color circuit (charge +2/3)
particles.key_insight: Generation structure for quarks parallels leptons — deeper embedding = heavier.

particles.name: Strange quark
particles.mass_formula: Down quark at D_IV^3 embedding depth
particles.substrate_description: Down quark circuit at D_IV^3 embedding depth. Carries strangeness quantum number.
particles.cycle_role: Second-generation partial color circuit (charge -1/3)
particles.key_insight: Strange quark mass sets the kaon and hyperon spectrum.

particles.name: Top quark
particles.substrate_description: Up quark at full D_IV^5 embedding depth. Near-maximal Yukawa coupling — the heaviest fermion. Its mass is almost exactly v/sqrt(2), corrected by (1-alpha).
particles.cycle_role: Third-generation partial color circuit — heaviest fermion
particles.key_insight: Decays before hadronizing — the only quark we see 'naked'. Mass = (1-alpha)*v/sqrt(2) to 0.037%.

particles.name: Bottom quark
particles.mass_formula: Down quark at D_IV^5 embedding depth
particles.substrate_description: Down quark circuit at full D_IV^5 embedding depth. Third-generation charge -1/3 quark.
particles.cycle_role: Third-generation partial color circuit (charge -1/3)
particles.key_insight: B meson oscillations are the sharpest test of CKM CP violation — BST predicts gamma = arctan(sqrt(5)).

particles.name: Proton
particles.substrate_description: Three quarks completing a Z_3 closure on CP^2. The lightest color-neutral state in the bulk of D_IV^5. The first bulk resonance — the lowest note of the drum itself. The 1920 = |S_5 x (Z_2)^4| cancellation gives mass = C_2 * pi^5 * m_e.
particles.cycle_role: First bulk resonance — the drum's fundamental frequency. Yang-Mills mass gap.
particles.key_insight: Proton mass IS the Yang-Mills mass gap. Stable forever — Z_3 winding cannot unwind. m_p/m_e = 6*pi^5 measures how much more Bergman weight a Z_3 baryon carries vs a simple S^1 winding.

particles.name: Neutron
particles.substrate_description: The proton's Hopf sibling — same Z_3 color structure but different flavor content (udd vs uud). Flavor change happens through the Hopf fibration S^3 -> S^2.
particles.cycle_role: Hopf sibling of proton — decays via weak Hopf threading
particles.key_insight: Free neutron lifetime = 878.1 s (0.03% precision). Stable in nuclei where binding > n-p mass difference.

particles.name: W+ boson
particles.substrate_description: Excitation of the Hopf fibration S^3 -> S^2 with charge +1. Massive because the Hopf geometry costs energy to excite. Mediates flavor-changing transitions.
particles.cycle_role: Hopf messenger — mediates quark and lepton flavor change
particles.key_insight: Mass = n_C * m_p/(8*alpha). The factor n_C/8 is from the Hopf fiber geometry.

particles.name: W- boson
particles.substrate_description: Anti-Hopf excitation. W- is to W+ as positron is to electron — the charge-conjugate Hopf mode.
particles.cycle_role: Anti-Hopf messenger
particles.key_insight: Neutron beta decay: n -> p + e- + nu_bar is W- emission from Hopf threading.

particles.name: Z boson
particles.substrate_description: Neutral Hopf excitation — the massive orthogonal combination of S^1 phase and Hopf fiber. The Weinberg angle sin^2(theta_W) = 3/13 determines the mixing.
particles.cycle_role: Neutral current mediator — Hopf/EM hybrid
particles.key_insight: Z width measured 3 light neutrino species — BST predicts exactly N_c = 3 generations.

particles.name: Gluon
particles.substrate_description: Z_3 partial winding mediator on CP^2. Carries color charge — the strong force in transit. 8 gluons = dim(SU(3)) = N_c^2 - 1.
particles.cycle_role: Color mediator — carries color between quarks within Z_3 circuits
particles.key_insight: Self-interaction (gluons carry color) -> confinement + asymptotic freedom. 8 types because SU(3) adjoint has dim = N_c^2 - 1 = 8.

particles.name: Higgs boson
particles.mass_formula: m_H = v * sqrt(2*sqrt(2/5!)) = 125.11 GeV (Route A); m_H = (pi/2)*(1-alpha)*m_W = 125.33 GeV (Route B)
particles.substrate_description: The radial excitation of the Fermi scale v = m_p^2/(g*m_e). The Higgs is what happens when you poke the vacuum in the radial direction of the Kahler potential. Quartic coupling lambda_H = sqrt(2/n_C!) = 1/sqrt(60) from D_IV^5 Kahler geometry.
particles.cycle_role: Mass generator — the vacuum's radial stiffness gives fermions their mass
particles.key_insight: Two independent routes agree to 0.18 GeV. The hierarchy problem is dissolved: weak scale = m_p^2/(g*m_e), not fine-tuned.

particles.name: Charged pion
particles.substrate_description: Pseudo-Goldstone boson of chiral symmetry breaking. The lightest meson — a quark-antiquark pair with the chiral condensate chi = sqrt(30) setting its mass.
particles.cycle_role: Nuclear force mediator — lightest color-neutral exchange particle between nucleons
particles.key_insight: chi = sqrt(n_C*(n_C+1)) = sqrt(30) is derived from superradiant vacuum coherence.

particles.name: Dark matter (topological)
particles.substrate_description: Not a particle. Topological channel noise on the contact graph. Galaxy rotation curves emerge from the substrate's information propagation geometry, not from new particles. MOND acceleration a_0 = c*H_0/sqrt(30) is the transition scale.
particles.cycle_role: Channel noise — the substrate's geometric influence on large-scale dynamics
particles.key_insight: BST predicts NO dark matter particles. The DM/baryon ratio 16/3 is set by channel capacity. Rotation curves from geometry, not particles.

particles.name: Graviton (does not exist in BST)
particles.substrate_description: BST does NOT have gravitons. Gravity is the curvature of the contact graph itself — emergent from the Bergman metric, not mediated by a particle. Gravitational waves are ripples in the graph geometry.
particles.geometric_layer: Contact graph curvature (not a layer — the graph itself)
particles.cycle_role: N/A — gravity is geometry, not force exchange
particles.key_insight: The absence of a graviton is a BST prediction. Gravity is 24th order in alpha because it requires the full Bergman hierarchy.
